name: Rose Hip Jam
recipeIngredient: 4 quarts Rose hips with black ends (about 5 pounds)
recipeInstructions:
1. Gather the rose hips after the first frost. Wash them well in case there is any insecticide residue.
2. Cover the rose hips with water and simmer until they are very soft and falling apart.
3. Press through a food mill or colander to remove the seeds and larger particles.
4. Press through a finer sieve to remove smaller fibers and seed bits.
5. Cook the pulp down until it is quite thick, thicker than heavy cream.
6. Add about one pound of sugar for every pound of pulp. Taste and adjust sweetness as desired.
7. Cook over high heat until the mixture reaches a thick jam-like consistency.
8. Pour into jars and store.
Note: Rose hips have enough pectin to jell and enough ascorbic acid to make it slightly tart.
